title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: vignette describes complete pipeline running AquaCrop simulations across Burkina Faso using gridded ERA5 data aquacropr package. AquaCrop (Steduto et al., 2009; Raes et al., 2009) FAO reference model simulating crop yield response water stress. package automates entire file-based workflow — input file generation, batch simulation, output parsing — always delegating computation official FAO binary, ensuring full scientific validity. workflow divided five steps: Project setup — Initialise AquaCrop directory structure process_climate — Preprocess ERA5 climate data build simulation grid process_soil — Extract soil properties HWSD write_inputs — Write AquaCrop input files batch run_aquacrop — Launch simulations

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: generic function processes four variables identically. reads NetCDF, clips shapefile extent, masks pixels outside country boundary, writes result GeoTIFF. ERA5 units: PCP (tp) ETO (evspsblpot) m/day raw ERA5 — multiply 1000 obtain mm/day. Temperature variables (t2m) already °C provided NetCDF files.
code: process_raster <- function(var_name, shp) {   input_file  <- sprintf("RAWDATA/climate/BF_%s_day.nc", var_name)   output_file <- sprintf("DATA/climate/BF_%s_day_msk.tif", var_name)    rast(input_file) |>     crop(shp) |>     mask(mask = shp) |>     writeRaster(filename = output_file, overwrite = TRUE)    message(sprintf("v %s processed", var_name)) }  walk(   .x = c("PCP", "TX", "TN", "ETO"),   .f = process_raster,   shp = shp )

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: Valid pixel coordinates masked TX raster define grid cell centres. pixel becomes sf point identified station name (grid_001, grid_002, …). Visualise grid country boundary. Run block R session (outside vignette) produce figure file:
code: pts <- rast("DATA/climate/BF_TX_day_msk.tif") |>   as_tibble(xy = TRUE) |>   drop_na() |>   select(x, y) |>   mutate(station = sprintf("grid_%03d", 1:nrow(.))) |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 4326)  # Square grid at 0.25° (ERA5 resolution) pts_grid <- st_grid(x = pts, cellsize = 0.25, square = TRUE)  write_sf(pts_grid, "DATA/shapefiles/pts_grid.shp") ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = pts_grid) +   geom_sf(data = pts) +   geom_sf(data = shp, fill = NA, color = "red", lwd = 0.5) +   coord_sf() +   labs(     title    = "ERA5 Grid (0.25 deg) - Burkina Faso",     subtitle = sprintf("%d simulation cells", nrow(pts))   )

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: raster pivoted long format. Band indices map actual dates 1981–2020 time sequence. helper function avoids repeating pivot logic variable: Apply variable join: output climate_grid_data.csv contains following columns:
code: dates <- seq.Date(   from = as.Date("1981-01-01"),   to   = as.Date("2020-12-31"),   by   = 1 ) pivot_raster <- function(tif_path, value_name, prefix_to_remove) {   rast(tif_path) |>     as_tibble(xy = TRUE) |>     drop_na() |>     mutate(station = sprintf("grid_%03d", 1:nrow(.)), .after = y) |>     pivot_longer(       cols      = -c(station, x, y),       names_to  = "date",       values_to = value_name     ) |>     mutate(       date  = str_remove_all(date, prefix_to_remove),       date  = dates[as.numeric(date)],       year  = year(date),       month = month(date),       day   = day(date),       .after = date     ) |>     select(-c(x, y, date)) } tn_df  <- pivot_raster("DATA/climate/BF_TN_day_msk.tif",  "tmin", "t2m_") tx_df  <- pivot_raster("DATA/climate/BF_TX_day_msk.tif",  "tmax", "t2m_") pcp_df <- pivot_raster("DATA/climate/BF_PCP_day_msk.tif", "rain", "tp_") eto_df <- pivot_raster("DATA/climate/BF_ETO_day_msk.tif", "et0",  "evspsblpot_")  weather_df <- left_join(tn_df, tx_df) |>   left_join(pcp_df) |>   left_join(eto_df)  weather_df |>   mutate(across(5:8, \(x) round(x, 1))) |>   write_csv("DATA/climate/climate_grid_data.csv")

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: Soil data come HWSD (Harmonized World Soil Database), pre-processed USDA textural class codes (1–12) West Africa. Hydraulic properties derived automatically texture via pedotransfer functions (Saxton & Rawls, 2006) inside write_sol_batch().

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: Two functions sourced SRC/hwsdb_helpers.R. num_to_usda(texture, ) converts HWSD numeric code (1–12) one three text formats: .get_mode(x) returns dominant (modal) texture value within grid cell. Ties broken returning first encountered value.
code: num_to_usda <- function(texture = 1, to = "abr") {   if (to == "abr") {     dplyr::case_when(       texture == 1  ~ "Cl",     texture == 2  ~ "SiCl",       texture == 3  ~ "SaCl",   texture == 4  ~ "ClLo",       texture == 5  ~ "SiClLo", texture == 6  ~ "SaClLo",       texture == 7  ~ "Lo",     texture == 8  ~ "SiLo",       texture == 9  ~ "SaLo",   texture == 10 ~ "Si",       texture == 11 ~ "LoSa",   texture == 12 ~ "Sa",       .default = NA_character_     )   } else if (to == "name_fr") {     dplyr::case_when(       texture == 1  ~ "argile",             texture == 2  ~ "argile limoneuse",       texture == 3  ~ "argile sableuse",    texture == 4  ~ "limon argileux",       texture == 5  ~ "limon argileux fin", texture == 6  ~ "limon argilo-sableux",       texture == 7  ~ "limon",              texture == 8  ~ "limon fin",       texture == 9  ~ "limon sableux",      texture == 10 ~ "limon tres fin",       texture == 11 ~ "sable limoneux",     texture == 12 ~ "sable",       .default = NA_character_     )   } else {     dplyr::case_when(       texture == 1  ~ "clay",            texture == 2  ~ "silty clay",       texture == 3  ~ "sandy clay",      texture == 4  ~ "clay loam",       texture == 5  ~ "silty clay loam", texture == 6  ~ "sandy clay loam",       texture == 7  ~ "loam",            texture == 8  ~ "silty loam",       texture == 9  ~ "sandy loam",      texture == 10 ~ "silt",       texture == 11 ~ "loamy sand",      texture == 12 ~ "sand",       .default = NA_character_     )   } } .get_mode <- function(x, ...) {   u   <- unique(x)   tab <- tabulate(match(x, u))   m   <- u[tab == max(tab, ...)]   if (length(m) > 1L) m <- m[1]   return(m) }

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: HWSD layer depths: Layers D6 D7 assigned 0.5 m thickness; layers 0.2 m. controls soil profile depth fed AquaCrop. output hwsd_grids.csv contains following columns:
code: grid_shp <- read_sf("DATA/shapefiles/pts_grid.shp")  soilc   <- rast("RAWDATA/soil/hwsd_wa_usda_all_layers.tif") |> crop(grid_shp) soil_df <- terra::extract(soilc, grid_shp, fun = .get_mode)  soil_df2 <- grid_shp |>   bind_cols(soil_df) |>   relocate(geometry, .after = everything()) |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   select(-ID) |>   mutate_if(is.double, num_to_usda, to = "name") |>   pivot_longer(     cols      = -station,     names_to  = "thickness",     values_to = "texture"   ) |>   mutate(     thickness = ifelse(thickness %in% c("D6", "D7"), 0.5, 0.2),     cn  = 46,     rew = 5   )  write_csv(soil_df2, "DATA/soil/hwsd_grids.csv")

text: Default parameters built AquaCrop crops. Override individual parameters via params = list(...), using AquaCrop variable identifier name.
code: write_cro(   path      = "CROP/",   crop_name = "maize90days",   params    = list(     var_05 = 1,   # Establishment method: 1 = sown     var_08 = 10   # Base temperature (deg C) below which development stops   ) ) # → CROP/maize90days.CRO

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: HWSD data converted list parameters per station. NA texture values (undefined pixels) replaced "impermeable".
code: params_list <- read_csv("DATA/soil/hwsd_grids.csv") |>   mutate(texture = replace_na(texture, "impermeable")) |>   group_split(station, .keep = FALSE) |>   map(as.list)  write_sol_batch(   site_name          = NULL,       # auto-discover all sites   params             = params_list,   write_sw0          = TRUE,   initial_water      = "WP",       # initial soil water content = wilting point   salinity           = "none",   initial_cc         = -9.00,   initial_biomass    = 0.000,   initial_root_depth = -9.00,   bund_water         = 0.0,   bund_ec            = 0.00,   swo_layers         = NULL,   clean              = TRUE ) # → SOIL/grid_XXX.SOL # → SOIL/grid_XXX.SW0

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: Field management parameters applied uniformly across grid cells.
code: write_man_batch(   site_name = NULL,   params    = list(     var_03 = 20,   # Mulch cover (%)     var_04 = 60,   # Evaporation reduction from mulch (%)     var_05 = 50    # Soil fertility level (%)   ),   clean = TRUE ) # → MANAGEMENT/grid_XXX.MAN

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: create_irr_schedule() builds schedule data frame defining trigger thresholds application depths successive crop periods, passed write_irr_batch().
code: schedule <- create_irr_schedule(   from_day        = c(1,   41,  116),   time_crit       = c(3,   7,   999),   # interval (days) per period   depth_crit      = c(10,  40,  0),     # application depth (mm) per period   ecw             = c(0.4, 0.6, 0.8),  # irrigation water EC (dS/m) per period   time_crit_code  = 1,                  # fixed interval   depth_crit_code = 2                   # fixed depth ) write_irr_batch(   path        = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name   = NULL,   method      = 1,    # 1 = sprinkler   wet_surface = 100,  # 100% wetted surface   mode        = 2,    # mode = generated schedule   irr_data    = schedule ) # → MANAGEMENT/grid_XXX.IRR

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: rainfed systems, planting dates typically depend seasonal rainfall onset. write_cal_batch() defines onset criterion; find_onset() called internally write_prm_batch() derive site-specific planting dates climate record.
code: write_cal_batch(   site_name     = NULL,   onset         = "rainfall",   window_start  = 121,   # search starts at DOY 121 (1 May)   window_length = 92,    # 92-day search window   criterion     = 4,     # cumulative rainfall criterion   preset_value  = 50,    # 50 mm threshold   occurrences   = 1 ) # → CAL/grid_XXX.CAL

title: Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacropr
text: Option — Fixed planting date (DOY 161, 10 June) applied uniformly: Option B — Onset-based planting date derived rainfall calendars (requires step 4.6): Fixed vs onset-based planting: DOY 161 appropriate regional analyses uniform sowing assumption. rainfed simulations account inter-annual variability season onset, use Option B together write_cal_batch().
code: plsch <- data.frame(year = 1981:2020, planting_doy = 161)  write_prm_batch(   site_name         = NULL,   crop_name         = "maize90days",   planting_schedule = plsch,   crop_duration     = 90,   clean             = TRUE ) # → LIST/grid_XXX.PRM write_prm_batch(   site_name     = NULL,   crop_name     = "maize90days",   calendar_path = "CAL/",   # onset calendars from step 4.6   crop_duration = 90,   clean         = TRUE ) # → LIST/grid_XXX.PRM

text: Sivakumar (1988) proposed one earliest operationally robust definitions onset West Africa: first day threshold date rainfall given number days meets minimum, provided prolonged dry spell follows within defined subsequent period. title: Onset of the Growing Season: Methods, Limitations, and Sahelian Realities
text: Marteau et al. (2009) refined Sivakumar definition using field observations actual sowing decisions pearl millet farmers southwest Niger. formulation adjusts rainfall dry spell thresholds based agronomic field evidence rather meteorological convention alone, anchoring onset definition observed farmer behaviour. particularly well suited operational use Sahelian belt.

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: AquaCrop FAO reference model simulating crop yield response water stress (Steduto et al., 2009; Raes et al., 2009). strong scientific foundation relatively low data requirements made reference tool research, operational studies, climate impact assessments worldwide. Running AquaCrop scale, however, can tedious. model relies file-based workflow — simulation requires manually preparing set formatted input files climate, soil, crop, irrigation, management parameters, invoking executable, finally parsing output files one one. becomes bottleneck soon simulations span multiple sites, years, scenarios. aquacropr solves providing lightweight R interface automates entire workflow. handles input file generation, batch simulation orchestration, output parsing — R, scriptable, compatible standard tidyverse spatial workflows. model engine never touched: aquacropr always delegates computation official FAO AquaCrop binary, ensuring full scientific validity consistency published results.

what: What you can do with aquacropr
title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: Generate input files programmatically — convert R data frames directly AquaCrop-compatible climate, soil, crop, irrigation, management files, manual editing. Run batch simulations scale — automate simulations across station networks, climate scenarios, planting date calendars, multi-year periods. Parse tidy outputs — read AquaCrop result files structured data frames ready analysis, visualization, export. Build reproducible workflows — script full chain raw data final results, enabling version control, traceability, collaboration.

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: aquacropr aimed agronomists, climate scientists, hydrologists, data analysts working crop-water-climate interactions — whether regional yield assessments, climate change impact studies, irrigation optimization, operational crop monitoring, research capacity building.

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: first step set dedicated project directory. init_aquacrop() creates standard AquaCrop folder structure downloads official FAO binary appropriate operating system. open default new clean RStudio project. Consult readme.txt understand directory structure. workflow walks complete single-station simulation: climate → crop → soil → management → irrigation → onset calendar → project file → run. Every step produces one AquaCrop-formatted files directly R objects, manual editing.
code: library(aquacropr)  init_aquacrop(path = "my-project", version = "7.2")

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: aquacropr ships built-weather dataset containing daily observations single station. Inspect first, single call write_climate() generates four climate files required AquaCrop. need finer control file content naming, individual writers (write_plu(), write_tnx(), write_eto(), write_cli()) also available.
code: head(weather) #>   year month day rain  et0 tmin tmax #> 1 1976     1   1  0.0 3.21  9.2 24.1 #> 2 1976     1   2  0.0 3.45 10.1 25.3 #> 3 1976     1   3  2.4 2.87  8.7 21.6  write_climate(   data      = weather,   site_name = "wakanda" ) # → CLIMATE/wakanda.PLU  (rainfall) # → CLIMATE/wakanda.Tnx  (min/max temperature) # → CLIMATE/wakanda.ETo  (reference evapotranspiration) # → CLIMATE/wakanda.CLI  (master file linking the three above)

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: write_cro() generates crop parameter file. Default parameters provided crops supported AquaCrop. Individual parameters can overridden via params = list(...), element named AquaCrop variable identifier.
code: write_cro(   crop_name = "maize",   params = list(     var_35 = 1.10,  # higher crop coefficient     var_50 = 0.85,  # maximum canopy cover     var_64 = 35     # reference harvest index   ) ) # → CROP/maize.CRO

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: write_sol() generates soil profile initial water content layer-wise descriptors. Pedotransfer functions (Saxton & Rawls, 2006) applied automatically derive hydraulic properties texture measured values unavailable. Salinity class curve number can specified per layer.
code: write_sol(   site_name     = "wakanda",   cn            = c(65, 70),   rew           = c(7, 5),   texture       = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   thickness     = c(0.4, 0.6),   initial_water = "WP",   salinity      = "very slight" ) # → SOIL/wakanda.SOL # → SOIL/wakanda.SW0

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: write_man() generates field management file. Soil fertility level, mulch cover, evaporation reduction mulch controlled via params.
code: write_man(   path      = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "wakanda",   params = list(     var_05 = 35,  # 35% fertility level     var_03 = 30,  # 30% mulch cover     var_04 = 50   # 50% evaporation reduction from mulch   ) ) # → MANAGEMENT/wakanda.MAN

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: aquacropr supports AquaCrop irrigation modes. automatic irrigation (mode 2), create_irr_schedule() first builds schedule data frame — defining trigger thresholds application depths successive crop periods — passed write_irr().
code: schedule_raw <- create_irr_schedule(   from_day        = c(1, 30, 60),   time_crit       = c(50, 40, 30),   # % RAW depletion threshold per period   depth_crit      = c(0, 0, 5),      # back to FC (+ 5 mm on last period)   ecw             = c(0.5, 0.6, 0.7),   time_crit_code  = 3,               # criterion: % RAW   depth_crit_code = 1                # application: back to field capacity )  write_irr(   path        = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name   = "wakanda",   method      = 5,   wet_surface = 30,   mode        = 2,   irr_data    = schedule_raw ) # → MANAGEMENT/wakanda.IRR

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: planting dates depend seasonal rainfall onset rather fixed calendar date — standard situation rainfed systems — write_cal() defines onset criterion. find_onset() called internally write_prm() derive planting dates year climate record.
code: write_cal(   site_name     = "wakanda",   onset         = "rainfall",   window_start  = 121,   # search starts at DOY 121 (May 1)   window_length = 92,    # 92-day search window   criterion     = 4,     # cumulative rainfall criterion   preset_value  = 50,    # 50 mm threshold   occurrences   = 1 ) # → CAL/wakanda.CAL

title: R interface to the FAO AquaCrop crop water productivity model (v7.0+)
text: write_prm() assembles inputs single .PRM file. calendar_path provided, find_onset() called internally derive planting dates climate series .CAL criterion — planting_schedule argument needed. Fixed planting dates can alternatively supplied directly data frame.
code: write_prm(   site_name       = "wakanda",   crop_name       = "maize",   irrigation_path = "MANAGEMENT",   calendar_path   = "CAL/"   # or, for fixed dates:   # planting_schedule = data.frame(year = 1976:2005, planting_doy = 121) ) # → LIST/wakanda.PRM

title: Soil Initial Water Content Default Values — SWOData
text: Default ECe values 0 dS/m (non-saline). Typical ECe ranges: 0-2 dS/m: Non-saline (crops unaffected) 2-4 dS/m: Slightly saline (sensitive crops affected) 4-8 dS/m: Moderately saline (many crops affected) 8-16 dS/m: Strongly saline (tolerant crops) >16 dS/m: strongly saline (crops tolerate)

what: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: Compute crop stage lengths calendar crop cycle length. function calculates duration different phenological stages based total crop cycle length, scaled reference 90-day cycle.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
code: calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 90)

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: crop_cycle Positive integer representing total length crop cycle days. Must whole number > 0. Default = 90 days.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: named list six integer values (var_52 var_57) representing crop stage durations days. list can passed directly write_cro() via params argument create crop file custom phenology.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
text: function calculates six crop stage variables based proportions reference 90-day crop cycle: var_52: Days sowing emergence (8/90 cycle = ~9%) var_53: Days sowing maximum canopy cover (100% cycle) var_54: Days sowing start canopy senescence (65/90 cycle = ~72%) var_55: Days sowing maturity (100% cycle) var_56: Days sowing start yield formation (52/90 cycle = ~58%) var_57: Duration yield formation period (20/90 cycle = ~22%) values rounded nearest integer day.

title: Get Crop Stages Lengths and Calendar — calculate_crop_stages
code: # Default 90-day cycle calculate_crop_stages() #> $var_52 #> [1] 8 #>  #> $var_53 #> [1] 90 #>  #> $var_54 #> [1] 65 #>  #> $var_55 #> [1] 90 #>  #> $var_56 #> [1] 52 #>  #> $var_57 #> [1] 20 #>  #> attr(,"description") #>                     var_52                     var_53  #>        "Days to emergence"   "Days to maximum canopy"  #>                     var_54                     var_55  #>       "Days to senescence"         "Days to maturity"  #>                     var_56                     var_57  #>  "Days to yield formation" "Yield formation duration"   # 120-day maize cycle maize_stages <- calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 120)  # Use with write_cro to create a crop file if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ stages <- calculate_crop_stages(crop_cycle = 130) write_cro(   path = "crop/",   crop_name = "maize-130d",   params = stages ) } # }

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Calculate number plants per hectare based plant spacing row spacing. useful determining planting density AquaCrop simulations field planning.

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
code: calculate_plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.3, row_spacing = 0.8)  plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.3, row_spacing = 0.8)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: plant_spacing Numeric. Distance plants within row (meters). Must positive (> 0). Default = 0.3 m row_spacing Numeric. Distance rows (meters). Must positive (> 0). Default = 0.8 m

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Integer representing number plants per hectare (plants/ha)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: calculation uses formula: $$plants/ha = \frac{10000}{plant\_spacing \times row\_spacing}$$ 10,000 m² = 1 hectare. result rounded (ceiling) ensure least calculated number plants per hectare.

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
text: Maize: Low density: 30,000-40,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.40m) Medium density: 50,000-70,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.20m) High density: 80,000-100,000 plants/ha (0.50m × 0.20m) Wheat: Typical: 250-400 plants/m² = 2,500,000-4,000,000 plants/ha Soybean: Low: 250,000-350,000 plants/ha (0.75m × 0.05m) High: 400,000-500,000 plants/ha (0.50m × 0.05m)

title: Calculate Plant Population Density — calculate_plant_density
code: # Default maize spacing (0.3m × 0.8m) calculate_plant_density() #> [1] 41667 # Returns: 41,667 plants/ha  # Using alias plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.40, row_spacing = 0.75) #> [1] 33334 # Returns: 33,334 plants/ha  # Dense maize planting calculate_plant_density(plant_spacing = 0.20, row_spacing = 0.60) #> [1] 83334 # Returns: 83,334 plants/ha  # Cotton spacing plant_density(0.15, 1.0) #> [1] 66667 # Returns: 66,667 plants/ha  # Calculate for multiple scenarios spacings <- data.frame(   plant_spacing = c(0.20, 0.30, 0.40),   row_spacing = c(0.75, 0.75, 0.75) ) spacings$plants_per_ha <- mapply(   calculate_plant_density,   spacings$plant_spacing,   spacings$row_spacing )

title: Create Irrigation Schedule Rules Data — create_irr_schedule
text: from_day Integer vector. Day sowing/planting rule valid. First value must 1. time_crit Numeric vector. Time criterion value. Meaning depends time_crit_code: Code 1: Fixed interval days irrigations Code 2: Allowable depletion mm irrigation Code 3: Allowable depletion % RAW irrigation depth_crit Numeric vector. Depth criterion value. Meaning depends depth_crit_code: Code 1: Extra water (mm) top amount reach FC (can 0, positive, negative) Code 2: Fixed net irrigation application depth mm ecw Numeric vector. Electrical conductivity irrigation water dS/m time_crit_code Integer. Time criterion type (applies rules): 1 = Fixed interval (days) 2 = Allowable depletion (mm water) 3 = Allowable depletion (% RAW) depth_crit_code Integer. Depth criterion type (applies rules): 1 = Back Field Capacity (optional adjustment) 2 = Fixed net application depth crop_length Integer. Optional. Length crop cycle days validation. provided, warns schedule rules starting beyond crop harvest.

text: Historical Observations: MaunaLoa.CO2: Atmospheric CO2 Mauna Loa Observatory (observed data) GlobalAverage.CO2: Global average atmospheric CO2 concentrations IPCC Scenarios: IPCC-BERN_A1B.CO2: IPCC BERN model A1B scenario RCP Scenarios (Representative Concentration Pathways - used IPCC AR5): RCP2-6.CO2: RCP 2.6 - Strong mitigation (radiative forcing peaks ~3 W/m² declines) RCP4-5.CO2: RCP 4.5 - Moderate mitigation (stabilizes ~4.5 W/m²) RCP6-0.CO2: RCP 6.0 - Intermediate pathway (stabilizes ~6 W/m²) RCP8-5.CO2: RCP 8.5 - High emissions (rises ~8.5 W/m² 2100) SSP Scenarios (Shared Socioeconomic Pathways - used IPCC AR6): SSP1_1.9.CO2: SSP1-1.9 - low emissions (~1.9 W/m²) SSP1_2.6.CO2: SSP1-2.6 - Low emissions (~2.6 W/m²) SSP2_4.5.CO2: SSP2-4.5 - Moderate emissions (~4.5 W/m²) SSP3_7.0.CO2: SSP3-7.0 - High emissions (~7.0 W/m²) SSP5_8.5.CO2: SSP5-8.5 - high emissions (~8.5 W/m²)

title: Get access to CO2 Files Bundled in aquacropr — path_to_file
code: # List all available CO2 files path_to_file() #>  [1] "AUTHORS.md"                    "AggregationResults.SIM"        #>  [3] "DailyResultsFullList.SIM"      "GlobalAverage.CO2"             #>  [5] "IPCC-BERN_A1B.CO2"             "LICENSE"                       #>  [7] "MaunaLoa.CO2"                  "ParticularResultsFullList.SIM" #>  [9] "RCP2-6.CO2"                    "RCP4-5.CO2"                    #> [11] "RCP6-0.CO2"                    "RCP8-5.CO2"                    #> [13] "SSP1_1.9.CO2"                  "SSP1_2.6.CO2"                  #> [15] "SSP2_4.5.CO2"                  "SSP3_7.0.CO2"                  #> [17] "SSP5_8.5.CO2"                   # Get path to historical Mauna Loa data (used by default in write_cli) co2_hist <- path_to_file("MaunaLoa.CO2")  # Get paths to climate scenario files co2_rcp45 <- path_to_file("RCP4-5.CO2") co2_ssp126 <- path_to_file("SSP1_2.6.CO2")  # Read CO2 data for analysis if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read historical CO2 concentrations hist_data <- readLines(path_to_file("MaunaLoa.CO2"))  # Compare different scenarios rcp26_data <- readLines(path_to_file("RCP2-6.CO2")) rcp85_data <- readLines(path_to_file("RCP8-5.CO2"))  # Note: Typically you use write_cli() instead, which handles this automatically: write_cli(stn = "Wakanda", scenario = "rcp45") # Automatically uses RCP4-5.CO2 write_cli(stn = "Wakanda", scenario = "hist") # Automatically uses MaunaLoa.CO2 } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
text: Creates AquaCrop calendar file (.CAL) defining onset growing period. Three onset types supported: fixed calendar day, rainfall-based criterion, thermal criterion.

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
code: write_cal(   site_name,   onset,   fixed_day = NULL,   window_start = NULL,   window_length = NULL,   criterion = NULL,   preset_value = NULL,   successive_days = NULL,   occurrences = 1L,   rday = NULL,   dspell = NULL,   lookahead_days = NULL,   min_weekly_rain = NULL,   spell_days = NULL,   cum_rain_upper = NULL,   wet_days_lower = NULL,   wet_days_upper = NULL,   dry_spell_lower = NULL,   dry_spell_upper = NULL,   fuzzy_threshold = NULL,   path = "CAL/",   description = NULL,   version = 7.1,   eol = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
text: site_name Character. Name CAL file (without extension). Typically station name. onset Character. Onset type: "fixed", "rainfall", "thermal". fixed_day Integer. Calendar day (1-366) onset growing period. Required onset "fixed". window_start Integer. Day-number (1-366) start time window. Required onset "rainfall" "thermal". window_length Integer. Length time window days. Required onset "rainfall" "thermal". criterion Integer. Criterion number within onset group. Rainfall criteria (onset = "rainfall"): 1 Cumulative rainfall since start window >= preset (mm). 2 Observed rainfall N successive days >= preset (mm). Requires successive_days. 3 10-day rainfall >= preset (mm). 4 10-day rainfall >= preset fraction 10-day ETo (percent). 5 Generalised Sivakumar: cumulative rainfall >= preset (mm) successive_days days, least rday rainy days, dry spell (consecutive dry days) >= dspell within following lookahead_days days. Requires successive_days, rday, dspell, lookahead_days. 6 Marteau (2009): first wet day (> 1 mm) 1 2 consecutive days receiving >= preset mm total, followed rolling spell_days-day period total rainfall < min_weekly_rain mm within next lookahead_days days. Requires successive_days (1 2), min_weekly_rain, spell_days, lookahead_days. 7 Fuzzy logic (Waongo et al., 2014): three-component index (cumulative rainfall x wet-day count x dry spell) >= fuzzy_threshold. Requires preset_value (cum_rain_lower), successive_days (accum_days), cum_rain_upper, wet_days_lower, wet_days_upper, dry_spell_lower, dry_spell_upper, fuzzy_threshold. Thermal criteria (onset = "thermal"): 1 Daily Tmin N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days. 2 Daily Tmean N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days. 3 Cumulative GDD N successive days >= preset (degC). Requires successive_days. 4 Cumulative GDD since start window >= preset (degC). preset_value Numeric. Threshold value criterion. Unit: mm rainfall criteria 1-3, 5, 6, 7; percent criterion 4; degC thermal criteria. criterion 7: lower bound cumulative rainfall (mm), .e. cum_rain_lower. successive_days Integer. Number consecutive days. Required rainfall criteria 2, 5, 6 (1 2 criterion 6), 7 (accum_days), thermal criteria 1, 2, 3. Default: NULL. occurrences Integer. Number times criterion must met onset triggered (1-3). Ignored criterion 7. Default: 1. rday Integer. Minimum number rainy days (> 0.1 mm) within successive_days window. Required criterion 5. Default: NULL. dspell Integer. Maximum dry spell length (consecutive dry days) allowed look-ahead window. Required criterion 5. Default: NULL. lookahead_days Integer. Length (days) look-ahead window. Required criteria 5 6. Classical values: 30 (Sivakumar), 20 (Marteau). Default: NULL. min_weekly_rain Numeric. Minimum total rainfall (mm) required rolling spell_days-day window within look-ahead; windows totals value considered dry spells. Required criterion 6. Classical Marteau value: 5 mm. Default: NULL. spell_days Integer. Length (days) rolling window used evaluate dry spell condition look-ahead. Required criterion 6. Classical Marteau value: 7 days. Default: NULL. cum_rain_upper Numeric. Upper bound cumulative rainfall (mm) fuzzy gamma_1 function. Required criterion 7. Must > preset_value. wet_days_lower Integer. Lower bound wet-day count fuzzy gamma_2 function. Required criterion 7. wet_days_upper Integer. Upper bound wet-day count fuzzy gamma_2 function. Required criterion 7. Must > wet_days_lower. dry_spell_lower Integer. Lower bound longest dry spell (days) fuzzy gamma_3 function. Required criterion 7. dry_spell_upper Integer. Upper bound longest dry spell (days) fuzzy gamma_3 function. Required criterion 7. Must > dry_spell_lower. fuzzy_threshold Numeric 0 1. Defuzzification threshold. Required criterion 7. path Output directory path CAL files. Default: "CAL/". description Character. Description first line. Default: auto-generated based onset type criterion. version Numeric. AquaCrop version number. Default: 7.1. eol End--line character style. One "windows", "linux", "macos". NULL (default), auto-detected system.

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
text: Invisibly returns output file path.

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
text: User-facing criterion numbers run 1 4 standard AquaCrop rainfall criteria, 5 generalised Sivakumar method, 6 Marteau (2009) method, 7 fuzzy logic method (Waongo et al., 2014). Thermal criteria run 1 4 (converted internally 11-14).

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
text: Waongo, M., Laux, P., Traoré, S.B., Sanon, M., & Kunstmann, H. (2014). crop model fuzzy rule based approach optimizing maize planting dates Burkina Faso, West Africa. Journal Applied Meteorology Climatology, 53(3), 598–613. doi:10.1175/JAMC-D-13-0116.1

title: Write AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File — write_cal
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fixed onset on day 212 write_cal(   site_name = "station_01",   onset     = "fixed",   fixed_day = 212 )  # Rainfall criterion 2: observed rainfall >= 30 mm in 3 successive days write_cal(   site_name       = "station_01",   onset           = "rainfall",   window_start    = 121,   window_length   = 92,   criterion       = 2,   preset_value    = 30,   successive_days = 3,   occurrences     = 1 )  # Rainfall criterion 5: generalised Sivakumar # >= 20 mm in 3 days, >= 1 rainy day, max 7 consecutive dry days in 30 write_cal(   site_name       = "station_01",   onset           = "rainfall",   window_start    = 121,   window_length   = 92,   criterion       = 5,   preset_value    = 20,   successive_days = 3,   rday            = 1,   dspell          = 7,   lookahead_days  = 30,   occurrences     = 1 )  # Rainfall criterion 6: Marteau (2009) # >= 20 mm in 1 or 2 days, no 7-day period with < 5 mm in next 20 days write_cal(   site_name        = "station_01",   onset            = "rainfall",   window_start     = 121,   window_length    = 92,   criterion        = 6,   preset_value     = 20,   successive_days  = 2,   min_weekly_rain  = 5,   spell_days       = 7,   lookahead_days   = 20,   occurrences      = 1 )  # Rainfall criterion 7: fuzzy logic (Waongo et al., 2014) write_cal(   site_name       = "station_01",   onset           = "rainfall",   window_start    = 121,   window_length   = 92,   criterion       = 7,   preset_value    = 20,   # cum_rain_lower   successive_days = 3,    # accum_days   cum_rain_upper  = 40,   wet_days_lower  = 1,   wet_days_upper  = 3,   dry_spell_lower = 7,   dry_spell_upper = 15,   fuzzy_threshold = 0.5 ) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ params <- data.frame(   site_name       = c("site_A", "site_B"),   onset           = "rainfall",   window_start    = 121L,   window_length   = 92L,   criterion       = 2L,   preset_value    = 30,   successive_days = 3L,   occurrences     = 1L,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) write_cal_batch(params)  # Fuzzy logic (criterion 7) for multiple sites params_fuz <- data.frame(   site_name       = c("site_C", "site_D"),   onset           = "rainfall",   window_start    = 121L,   window_length   = 92L,   criterion       = 7L,   preset_value    = 20,   # cum_rain_lower   successive_days = 3L,   # accum_days   cum_rain_upper  = 40,   wet_days_lower  = 1L,   wet_days_upper  = 3L,   dry_spell_lower = 7L,   dry_spell_upper = 15L,   fuzzy_threshold = 0.5,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) write_cal_batch(params_fuz) } # }

Default = "weather/" (relative current working directory). See write_plu, write_eto, write_tnx, write_cli details. site_name Station name identifier. NULL (default), eol auto-detected. syear Start year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL. title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
text: x Data frame write file. columns converted character format. Factor columns automatically converted character writing. file Character string specifying output file path. Can relative absolute path. width Numeric value vector specifying character width column: Single value: columns use width (e.g., width = 10) Vector: column uses corresponding width (e.g., width = c(10, 15, 8)) single value provided multi-column data frame, recycled columns. justify Character string specifying text alignment within column width: "l": Left-align columns "r": Right-align columns Multi-character string: Align column individually (e.g., "lrl" left, right, left alignment three columns) Default = "l". single character provided multi-column data frame, recycled columns. replace_na Character string use place NA values. Default = "NA" eol End--line character style. Options: "windows","linux", "macos". NULL (default), eol auto-detected. "windows": Windows-style line endings (\r\n) "unix", "linux", "macOS": Unix-style line endings (\n) Default = "windows" append Logical. TRUE, append existing file. FALSE, overwrite existing file. Default = TRUE

title: Write Fixed-Width Format File — write_fwf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create sample data df <- data.frame(   year = c(2020, 2021, 2022),   rainfall = c(850.5, 920.3, 780.1),   temp = c(25.2, 26.1, 24.8) )  # Write with uniform width, left-aligned write_fwf(   x = df,   file = "output.txt",   width = 10,   justify = "l",   append = FALSE )  # Write with different widths per column, right-aligned write_fwf(   x = df,   file = "output.txt",   width = c(6, 10, 10),   justify = "r",   append = FALSE )  # Write with mixed alignment (left, right, right) write_fwf(   x = df,   file = "output.txt",   width = c(6, 10, 10),   justify = "lrr",   append = FALSE )  # Append to existing file write_fwf(   x = df[1:2, ],   file = "output.txt",   width = 10,   justify = "r",   append = TRUE )  # Handle NA values with custom replacement df_na <- data.frame(   year = c(2020, 2021, NA),   value = c(100, NA, 150) )  write_fwf(   x = df_na,   file = "output.txt",   width = 10,   justify = "r",   replace_na = "-9999",   append = FALSE )  # Unix-style line endings write_fwf(   x = df,   file = "output.txt",   width = 10,   justify = "r",   eol = "unix",   append = FALSE ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
title: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
text: Creates AquaCrop groundwater table file (.GWT) describing depth salinity groundwater table. Three modes supported: groundwater table (code 0), constant depth salinity (code 1), variable depth salinity time (code 2).

title: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
code: write_gwt(   site_name,   code,   gwt_data = NULL,   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   start_day = 1,   start_month = 1,   start_year = 1901,   description = NULL,   version = 7.1,   eol = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
text: site_name Character. Name GWT file (without extension). Typically station name. code Integer. Groundwater table mode: 0 = groundwater table, 1 = constant depth salinity, 2 = variable depth salinity. gwt_data code 0: ignored, can NULL. code 1: data.frame one row columns: day Integer. Day number (must 1 constant mode). depth Numeric. Depth soil surface meters. ecw Numeric. Electrical conductivity dS/m. code 2: data.frame columns: day Integer. Day number reference date. depth Numeric. Depth soil surface meters. ecw Numeric. Electrical conductivity dS/m. path Output directory path GWT files. Default: "MANAGEMENT/". start_day Integer. First day observations (code 2 ). Default: 1. start_month Integer. First month observations (code 2 ). Default: 1. start_year Integer. First year observations (code 2 ). Use 1901 linked specific year. Default: 1901. description Character. Description written first line. Default: auto-generated based code data. version Numeric. AquaCrop version number. Default: 7.1. eol End--line character style. One "windows", "linux", "macos". NULL (default), auto-detected system.

title: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
text: Invisibly returns output file path.

title: Write AquaCrop Groundwater Table (.GWT) File — write_gwt
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Code 0: no groundwater table write_gwt(site_name = "station_01", code = 0)  # Code 1: constant depth and salinity write_gwt(   site_name = "station_01",   code     = 1,   gwt_data = data.frame(day = 1, depth = 1.50, ecw = 1.5) )  # Code 2: variable depth and salinity write_gwt(   site_name    = "station_01",   code        = 2,   gwt_data    = data.frame(     day   = c(50, 100, 200, 300),     depth = c(1.00, 2.00, 3.00, 1.50),     ecw   = c(1.0,  2.0,  3.0,  1.7)   ),   start_day   = 1,   start_month = 1,   start_year  = 2000 ) } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
text: Creates AquaCrop v7.0+ irrigation (.IRR) file defines irrigation management simulation. irrigation file specifies irrigation method, percentage soil surface wetted, irrigation scheduling approach.

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
code: write_irr(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name,   method,   wet_surface,   mode,   irr_data,   version = 7.1,   eol = NULL,   crop_length = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
text: path Character. Directory path .IRR file created. directory created automatically exist. Default: "MANAGEMENT/". site_name Character. Name identifier irrigation scenario. name used filename (e.g., "drip-irrigation" creates "drip-irrigation.IRR"). method Integer. Irrigation method: 1 = Sprinkler irrigation 2 = Surface irrigation: Basin 3 = Surface irrigation: Border 4 = Surface irrigation: Furrow 5 = Drip irrigation wet_surface Integer. Percentage soil surface wetted irrigation (0-100). See Details typical values irrigation method. mode Integer. Irrigation mode: 1 = Specification irrigation events 2 = Generation irrigation schedule 3 = Determination net irrigation water requirement irr_data Data.frame numeric. Irrigation data. Structure depends mode: Mode 1: data.frame columns day, depth, ecw. See create_irr_events() Mode 2: data.frame columns from_day, time_crit, depth_crit, ecw attributes time_crit_code, depth_crit_code. See create_irr_schedule() Mode 3: Single numeric value representing allowable depletion RAW (%, 0-100) version Numeric. AquaCrop version. Default: 7.1 eol Character. End--line character style. Options: "windows", "linux", "macos". NULL (default), eol auto-detected. crop_length Integer. Optional. Length crop cycle days validation. provided, warns irrigation events beyond crop harvest.

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
text: Invisibly returns full path created .IRR file character string. main effect writing file disk.

what: Typical Wetted Surface by Irrigation Method
title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
text: wet_surface parameter represents percentage soil surface wetted irrigation. Typical values irrigation method:

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
text: Mode 1 - Specified Events: Define exact irrigation dates, depths, water quality. Use create_irr_events() create properly formatted data. Mode 2 - Generated Schedule: Define rules generate irrigation events automatically based time depletion criteria. Use create_irr_schedule() create properly formatted data required attributes. Mode 3 - Net Requirement: Calculate total irrigation water needed maintain soil water specified threshold (% RAW). Provide single numeric value.

title: Write AquaCrop Irrigation File — write_irr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example 1: Fixed irrigation schedule (Mode 1) events <- create_irr_events(   day = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),   depth = c(50, 50, 50, 50, 50),   ecw = c(1.0, 1.0, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6) )  write_irr(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "ouaga-fixed",   method = 5,        # Drip irrigation   wet_surface = 30,  # 30% surface wetted   mode = 1,          # Specified events   irr_data = events,   crop_length = 120  # Optional: validate against 120-day cycle )  # Example 2: Generated schedule with fixed interval (Mode 2) schedule <- create_irr_schedule(   from_day = c(1, 41, 116),   time_crit = c(999, 7, 999),      # 7-day interval from day 41-115   depth_crit = c(0, 40, 0),        # 40mm application depth   ecw = c(0.4, 0.6, 0.8),   time_crit_code = 1,              # Fixed interval   depth_crit_code = 2              # Fixed depth )  write_irr(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "bobo-schedule",   method = 1,         # Sprinkler   wet_surface = 100,  # 100% surface wetted   mode = 2,           # Generated schedule   irr_data = schedule )  # Example 3: RAW-based automatic irrigation (Mode 2) schedule_raw <- create_irr_schedule(   from_day = c(1, 30, 60),   time_crit = c(50, 40, 30),       # % RAW depletion threshold   depth_crit = c(0, 0, 5),         # Back to FC (+ 5mm on last period)   ecw = c(0.5, 0.6, 0.7),   time_crit_code = 3,              # % RAW criterion   depth_crit_code = 1              # Back to field capacity )  write_irr(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "auto-raw",   method = 5,   wet_surface = 30,   mode = 2,   irr_data = schedule_raw )  # Example 4: Net irrigation requirement (Mode 3) write_irr(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "koudougou-netreq",   method = 1,         # Sprinkler   wet_surface = 100,   mode = 3,           # Net requirement   irr_data = 50       # Do not deplete below 50% RAW ) } # }

text: function creates colon-delimited text file compatible AquaCrop v7.0+: Filename: <site_name>.MAN Format: value : description parameter separate line Header contains management scenario name key parameters

title: Write AquaCrop Management File — write_man
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example 1: Use all default values write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "default" )  # Example 2: High fertility with moderate mulching # See ?ManData for parameter details write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "high-fertility",   params = list(     var_05 = 10, # Low fertility stress (high fertility)     var_03 = 30, # 30% mulch cover     var_04 = 50 # 50% evaporation reduction   ) )  # Example 3: Low fertility, no mulch write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "low-fertility",   params = list(     var_05 = 70, # High fertility stress (poor soil)     var_03 = 0, # No mulch     var_04 = 0 # No mulch effect   ) )  # Example 4: Weed competition scenario # Check ?ManData for weed-related parameters write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "with-weeds",   params = list(     var_05 = 50, # Moderate fertility stress     var_09 = 20, # Weed cover at closure     var_10 = 10, # Weed increase in mid-season     var_12 = 50 # Weed replacement   ) )  # Example 5: Multiple cutting management (e.g., forage) write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "multiple-cuts",   params = list(     var_13 = 1, # Enable multiple cuttings     var_14 = 10, # Canopy after cut     var_15 = 20, # CGC increase after cut     var_16 = 45, # First cutting day     var_17 = 30 # Cutting window   ) )  # Example 6: Complete management scenario # Consult ?ManData to understand each parameter irrigation_params <- list(   var_03 = 40, # Mulch cover   var_04 = 70, # Mulch effect   var_05 = 20, # Fertility stress   var_06 = 0.15, # Bund height   var_07 = 10, # Runoff not affected   var_08 = 80 # Runoff prevented )  write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = "irrigated-field",   params = irrigation_params,   eol = "linux" )  # Example 7: Create station-specific management station <- "grid_001" write_man(   path = "MANAGEMENT/",   site_name = station,   params = list(var_05 = 35) ) } # }

Default: 0. mulch_after Integer. Percentage ground surface covered mulches growing period (0-100). Default: 0. mulch_effect Integer. Effect mulches reduction soil evaporation percent. Indicative values: 100 synthetic plastic mulches, 50 organic mulches, 10-100 user-specified. Default: 50. ecw_before Numeric. Electrical conductivity irrigation water growing period (dS/m). Default: 0. ecw_after Numeric. Electrical conductivity irrigation water growing period (dS/m). Default: 0. wet_surface Integer. Percentage soil surface wetted -season irrigation (0-100). Default: 100. irr_before data.frame NULL. Irrigation events growing period. Must columns: day Integer. Number days start simulation period. depth Integer. Net irrigation application depth mm. Maximum 5 rows. Default: NULL (events). irr_after data.frame NULL. Irrigation events growing period. Must columns: day Integer. Number days end growing period. depth Integer. Net irrigation application depth mm. Maximum 5 rows. Default: NULL (events). path Output directory path files. Default: "MANAGEMENT/". description Character. Description written first line. Default: "Field irrigation management conditions -season". version Numeric. AquaCrop version number. Default: 7.1. eol End--line character style. One "windows", "linux", "macos". NULL (default), auto-detected system.

title: Write AquaCrop Off-Season Conditions (.OFF) File — write_off
text: Invisibly returns output file path.

title: Write AquaCrop Off-Season Conditions (.OFF) File — write_off
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # No mulches, one irrigation event before season write_off(   site_name   = "station_01",   mulch_after = 70,   mulch_effect = 50,   ecw_before = 1.5,   ecw_after  = 3.0,   irr_before = data.frame(day = 10, depth = 40) )  # Mulches and irrigation events both before and after write_off(   site_name     = "station_01",   mulch_before = 50,   mulch_after  = 70,   mulch_effect = 100,   ecw_before   = 1.5,   ecw_after    = 3.0,   irr_before   = data.frame(day = c(5, 10), depth = c(30, 40)),   irr_after    = data.frame(day = c(5),     depth = c(25)) ) } # }

Options: "WP" "wilting_point": Sets wilting point based soil texture "FC" "field_capacity": Sets field capacity (1.5 × WP) "SAT" "saturation": Sets saturation (2.5 × WP) Numeric value: Sets layers specified value (0-100%) used layers parameter provided. Default = "WP" salinity Character string numeric specifying soil salinity level layers. Options: "none", "slight", "slight", "moderate", "strong", "strong", numeric ECe value (0-100 dS/m). used layers parameter provided. Default = "none" initial_cc Numeric. Initial canopy cover can reached without water stress (%). Use -9.00 automatic calculation AquaCrop. Default = -9.00 initial_biomass Numeric. Biomass produced simulation start (ton/ha). Default = 0.000 initial_root_depth Numeric. Initial effective rooting depth without water stress (m). Use -9.00 automatic calculation. Default = -9.00 bund_water Numeric. Water layer stored soil bunds present (mm). Default = 0.0 bund_ec Numeric. Electrical conductivity water bunds (dS/m). Default = 0.00 swo_layers Optional data.frame pass layers write_swo(). NULL (default), write_swo() construct layers texture thickness.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol
text: .SOL file defines hydraulic physical parameters soil horizon, including: SAT: Saturation (vol %) FC: Field capacity (vol %) WP: Wilting point (vol %) Ksat: Saturated hydraulic conductivity (mm day⁻¹) Penetrability, Gravel, CRa, CRb: Additional physical characteristics number horizons inferred lengths texture thickness.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol
text: write_sw0 = TRUE, write_swo() called using path, site_name (soil_name), texture, thickness (layer_thickness), eol. following arguments passed : initial_water, salinity, initial_cc, initial_biomass, initial_root_depth, bund_water, bund_ec, swo_layers (layers).

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File (.SOL) — write_sol
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic soil profile (single horizon) write_sol(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "field1",   texture = "loam",   thickness = 1.0 )  # Two horizons with automatic SW0 generation (field capacity) write_sol(   path = "soil/",   site_name = "layered",   cn = 65,   rew = 7,   texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   thickness = c(0.4, 0.6),   initial_water = "FC",   salinity = "none" )  # Write only the .SOL file (no SW0) write_sol(   site_name = "soil_only",   texture = "loam",   thickness = 1.0,   write_sw0 = FALSE ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch — write_sol_batch

title: Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch — write_sol_batch
code: write_sol_batch(   site_name = NULL,   params = NULL,   path = "SOIL/",   eol = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   clean = FALSE,   write_sw0 = TRUE,   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "none",   initial_cc = -9,   initial_biomass = 0,   initial_root_depth = -9,   bund_water = 0,   bund_ec = 0,   swo_layers = NULL,   climate_path = "CLIMATE/",   base_path = getwd() )

title: Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch — write_sol_batch
text: site_name Character vector NULL. Names sites process: NULL, sites automatically discovered .CLI files CLIMATE/ directory. vector, specified sites processed. params Either: single named list soil parameters (cn, rew, texture, thickness), applied sites, list named lists (one per site), containing soil parameters. length list must match number sites. Set NULL list() use default values sites. Valid parameters: cn, rew, texture, thickness. path Output directory path. Default: "SOIL/". eol End--line character style. Options: "windows","linux", "macos". NULL (default), eol auto-detected. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), prints progress messages. FALSE, runs silently. clean Logical. TRUE, removes existing .SOL .SW0 files path writing new files. Default: FALSE write_sw0 Logical; TRUE, also write SW0 files. Default: TRUE. initial_water Initial water content specification SW0 files. Default: "WP". salinity Salinity specification SW0 files. Default: "none". initial_cc Initial canopy cover SW0 files. Default: -9.00. initial_biomass Initial biomass SW0 files. Default: 0.000. initial_root_depth Initial root depth SW0 files. Default: -9.00. bund_water Bund water SW0 files. Default: 0.0. bund_ec Bund EC SW0 files. Default: 0.00. swo_layers Optional layers SW0 files. Default: NULL. climate_path Path climate file directory auto-discovery. Default: "CLIMATE/". base_path Base absolute path. Default: current working directory.

title: Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch — write_sol_batch

SW0-related parameters (initial_water, salinity, etc.) passed direct arguments write_sol_batch() applied sites. See write_sol documentation parameter descriptions valid ranges. Use function need create SOL files multiple sites .

title: Write AquaCrop SOL files in batch — write_sol_batch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example 1: Multiple sites, same soil profile soil_params <- list(   texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),   thickness = c(0.4, 0.6),   cn = 65,   rew = 7 ) sites <- c("grid_001", "grid_002", "grid_003") write_sol_batch(   site_name = sites,   params = soil_params )  # Example 2: Multiple sites, different soil profiles params_list <- list(   list(texture = "sandy loam", thickness = 1.0, cn = 55, rew = 5),   list(texture = "loam", thickness = 1.0, cn = 70, rew = 6),   list(texture = c("clay", "clay loam"), thickness = c(0.3, 0.7), cn = 80, rew = 7) ) write_sol_batch(   site_name = sites,   params = params_list,   path = "SOIL/",   initial_water = "FC",   salinity = "none" )  # Example 3: Auto-discover all sites, default soil parameters write_sol_batch(   site_name = NULL,   params = NULL,   base_path = "/my/project/path",   verbose = TRUE ) } # }

what: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) higher soil initial water content (.SW0) file specifies initial water content soil layer start simulation. file defines soil moisture status planting simulation start.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
code: write_swo(   path = "SOIL/",   soil_name = "default-soil-init",   texture = "loam",   eol = NULL,   initial_cc = -9,   initial_biomass = 0,   initial_root_depth = -9,   bund_water = 0,   bund_ec = 0,   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "none",   layer_thickness = 1,   layers = NULL )  write_sw0(   path = "SOIL/",   soil_name = "default-soil-init",   texture = "loam",   eol = NULL,   initial_cc = -9,   initial_biomass = 0,   initial_root_depth = -9,   bund_water = 0,   bund_ec = 0,   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "none",   layer_thickness = 1,   layers = NULL )

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: path Directory path .SW0 file written. Default = "SOIL/" soil_name Name identifier soil initial conditions file (used filename). Default = "default-soil-init" texture Character vector soil texture names layer. Valid textures: "sand", "loamy sand", "sandy loam", "loam", "silt loam", "silt", "sandy clay loam", "clay loam", "silty clay loam", "sandy clay", "silty clay", "clay". Default = "loam" eol End--line character style. Options: "windows","linux", "macos". NULL (default), eol auto-detected. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" initial_cc Numeric. Initial canopy cover can reached without water stress (%). Use -9.00 automatic calculation AquaCrop. Default = -9.00 initial_biomass Numeric. Biomass produced simulation start (ton/ha). Default = 0.000 initial_root_depth Numeric. Initial effective rooting depth without water stress (m). Use -9.00 automatic calculation. Default = -9.00 bund_water Numeric. Water layer stored soil bunds present (mm). Default = 0.0 bund_ec Numeric. Electrical conductivity water bunds (dS/m). Default = 0.00 initial_water Character string numeric. Specifies initial water content layers. Options: "WP" "wilting_point": Sets wilting point based soil texture "FC" "field_capacity": Sets field capacity (1.5 × WP) "SAT" "saturation": Sets saturation (2.5 × WP) Numeric value: Sets layers specified value (0-100%) used layers parameter provided. Default = "WP" salinity Character string numeric specifying soil salinity level layers. Options: "none", "slight", "slight", "moderate", "strong", "strong", numeric ECe value (0-100 dS/m). used layers parameter provided. Default = "none" layer_thickness Numeric vector specifying thickness soil layer meters. used layers parameter provided. Default = 1.0 (single 1m layer) layers Data frame soil layer information. provided, overrides initial_water. Must contain columns: thickness: Layer thickness meters (m) water_content: Volumetric water content (vol%) ece: Electrical conductivity (dS/m). Optional, defaults 0

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Invisibly returns full path created .SW0 file.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: .SW0 file defines initial soil water conditions layer start AquaCrop simulation. conditions significantly affect early crop growth development.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Canopy cover: Set -9.00 let AquaCrop calculate, specify actual value (0-100%) Biomass: Usually 0 planting, can specify pre-existing biomass Root depth: Set -9.00 automatic, specify initial depth meters

what: Soil Textures and Default Wilting Points:
title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: function uses SWOData dataset contains wilting point values 12 standard USDA soil textures. See data(SWOData) details.

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Wilting Point (WP): Minimum water plant survival (texture-specific) Field Capacity (FC): WP × 1.5 (approximate) Saturation (SAT): WP × 2.5 (approximate)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
text: Use salinity_to_ece convert qualitative descriptions ECe values: "none": 0 dS/m (non-saline) "slight": 3 dS/m (slightly saline) "moderate": 6 dS/m (moderately saline) "strong": 12 dS/m (strongly saline) "strong": 20 dS/m (strongly saline)

title: Write AquaCrop Soil Initial Water Content File — write_swo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple: Single layer at wilting point, no salinity write_swo(   path = "soil/",   soil_name = "loam-wp",   texture = "loam",   initial_water = "WP" )  # Single layer at field capacity write_swo(   soil_name = "sandy-loam-fc",   texture = "sandy loam",   initial_water = "FC" )  # With salinity write_swo(   soil_name = "saline-soil",   texture = "clay loam",   initial_water = "WP",   salinity = "moderate" )  # Multiple layers with different textures write_swo(   soil_name = "layered-profile",   texture = c("sandy loam", "loam", "clay loam"),   layer_thickness = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   initial_water = "FC",   salinity = "slight" )  # Mid-season with existing crop write_swo(   soil_name = "mid-season-maize",   texture = "loam",   initial_water = "FC",   initial_cc = 60,   initial_biomass = 3.5,   initial_root_depth = 0.7 )  # Custom numeric values write_swo(   soil_name = "custom",   texture = "loam",   layer_thickness = c(0.5, 0.5),   initial_water = 28,   salinity = 4.5 )  # Manual layers (full control) layers <- data.frame(   thickness = c(0.3, 0.4, 0.3),   water_content = c(20, 25, 30),   ece = c(0, 3, 6) )  write_swo(   soil_name = "manual-layers",   texture = c("Sandy Loam", "Loam", "Clay Loam"),   layers = layers ) } # }

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: Write AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) temperature (.Tnx) file containing daily minimum maximum temperature data use crop simulations.

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
code: write_tnx(   path = "CLIMATE/",   site_name = NULL,   data = NULL,   var_name_min = "tmin",   var_name_max = "tmax",   syear = NULL,   eyear = NULL,   eol = NULL,   record_type = 1,   first_day = 1,   first_month = 1 )

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: path Directory path output .Tnx file written. Default = "CLIMATE/" site_name Station name identifier weather station. Default = NULL (extracted data available) data Data frame containing daily temperature data. Can either: Columns: station, year, month, day, tmin, tmax (legacy format), Columns: year, month, day, tmin, tmax (new format) var_name_min Name minimum temperature column data frame. Default = "tmin" var_name_max Name maximum temperature column data frame. Default = "tmax" syear Start year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eyear End year data period (extracted data provided). Default = NULL eol End--line character style output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", "macOS". Default = "windows" record_type Type temporal aggregation. Options: 1 = daily, 2 = 10-daily, 3 = monthly. Default = 1 first_day First day record. 10-daily: 1, 11, 21; monthly: 1. Default = 1 first_month First month record (1-12). Default = 1

title: Write AquaCrop Temperature File — write_tnx
text: Invisibly returns full path created .Tnx file. Creates formatted .Tnx file specified directory format: <<site_name>>.Tnx containing daily minimum maximum temperature data required AquaCrop v7.0.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: Package renamed aquacroptools aquacropr. Update library() calls imports accordingly. read_cal() re-implemented standalone improved function (previously part readers.R). Dependency snakecase removed; withr added.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: install_source() — compile install AquaCrop Fortran source code (cross-platform). build_source() — build AquaCrop binary source. download_source() — download AquaCrop source code.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: find_onset() — detect rainy season onset using fuzzy logic algorithm. See vignette("sowingdate") full worked example.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: write_cal() / write_cal_batch() — write AquaCrop calendar (.CAL) files, single batch. write_gwt() / write_gwt_batch() — write groundwater table (.GWT) files. write_irr() / write_irrig_batch() — write irrigation schedule (.IRR) files. write_obs() / write_obs_batch() — write field observation (.OBS) files. write_off() / write_off_batch() — write -season condition files. write_ppn() — write plot/project parameter files. write_sim() — write simulation settings files. create_irr_events() / create_irr_schedule() — helper functions build irrigation event data frames.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: read_cal() — read AquaCrop calendar files. read_day_out() — read AquaCrop daily output files tibble. read_season_out() now returns tibble instead plain data frame.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: is_cli(), is_eto(), is_tnx() — validate climate input file formats.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: write_prm() / write_prm_batch() — major overhaul: dynamic optional file passing (SW0, GWT, IRR), improved day--year handling, calendar file integration via find_onset() + read_cal(), better warnings missing optional files. write_climate() — default output path changed "CLIMATE/". write_cal_batch() — additional parameters added finer control. write_fwf() — auto-detects EOF NULL passed. install_binaries() — fixed cross-platform behavior; version 7.3 (typo-tagged release) now excluded. init_aquacrop() — improved startup messaging initialization reliability. read_fwf() — output coerced numeric single-column results. Internal codebase refactored monolithic readers.R focused modules: read_inputs.R, read_outputs.R, read_cal.R, utils-batch.R, utils-climate.R, utils-readers.R, utils-validation.R, utils-misc.R.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: Fixed regex escaping internal clean-directory utility (#internal). Fixed crop duration handling associated warnings write_cro(). Fixed section header indentation write_prm(). Fixed edge-case crash find_onset(). Fixed install_source() cross-platform compilation.

title: aquacropr 0.2.0
text: Three new vignettes: settingup, sowingdate, regionalsim. pkgdown website updated rebuilt. README substantially revised reflect new package name capabilities. Repository URLs updated https://github.com/mwaongo/aquacropr.

title: aquacropr 0.1.0
text: Initial release aquacroptools. Core writers: write_cli(), write_eto(), write_plu(), write_tnx(), write_sol(), write_swo(), write_man(), write_man_batch(), write_sol_batch(), write_cro(), write_climate(), write_prm(), write_prm_batch(). Core readers: read_cli(), read_eto(), read_plu(), read_tnx(). install_binaries() — download install pre-built AquaCrop binaries. init_aquacrop() — initialize AquaCrop project directory. run_aquacrop() — run AquaCrop simulation. Helper utilities: build_crop_parameters(), calculate_crop_stages(), calculate_plant_density(), day_number(), to_aquacrop_day(), weather(), round_to(), ece_to_salinity(), salinity_to_ece().
